{{Quote|Well, the kid must've seen something important, or Skywalker wouldn't be sending us half-way across the galaxy to deliver him to his people.|A Rebel Alliance agent|<ref name="TRG1" />}}
'''Rhamsis Callo''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Danjar sector/Legends|Danjar sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and militaristic [[Schenor]] [[Species/Legends|species]], which allowed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to construct a [[Unidentified Imperial base (Rhamsis Callo)|base]] on the planet despite remaining neutral in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. During that conflict, a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents delivered a [[A'kazz|young survivor]] of an Imperial [[Massacre at the Schenor research station|massacre]] of a [[Schenor research station|Schenor colony]] to Rhamsis Callo. When the Schenor [[Noble/Legends|noble]] [[B'karitz]] learned about the massacre, he led an insurrection that attempted to drive the Imperial forces off Rhamsis Callo.

==Description==
Rhamsis Callo, also known simply as Callo, was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TRG1" /> located in the [[Rhamsis Callo system/Legends|Rhamsis Callo system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Danjar sector/Legends|Danjar sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the [[Schenor]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of Rhamsis Callo maintained their neutrality. However, the [[Schenor government]] did sign a [[Schenor/Imperial treaty|treaty]] with the Empire allowing the latter to begin the construction of a [[Unidentified Imperial base (Rhamsis Callo)|base]] on the planet. At some point during that conflict, a group of Rebel agents rescued the [[Youngling/Legends|young]] Schenor [[A'kazz]] from a [[Massacre at the Schenor research station|massacre]] the Empire carried out at a [[Schenor research station]]. The Rebels transported the child to a nearby Alliance base, where [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], believing that A'kazz's testimony of the Empire's actions could help persuade the latter's species to align with the Rebellion, subsequently tasked the group with delivering the young Schenor to his [[nest-guild]] on Rhamsis Callo.<ref name="TRG1" />

[[File:LukeESB-OP.png|thumb|left|250px|Commander Luke Skywalker (pictured) tasked a group of Rebel Alliance agents with transporting a Schenor child to Rhamsis Callo.]]
Arriving on the planet, the Rebel team traveled on a local [[reaction car]] to the province where A'kazz's [[nest-guildmaster]], [[B'karitz]], resided. During the journey, Imperial agents attempted to waylay the Rebels by staging vehicular chases, stealthy attacks, and [[Lightfight|shootouts]] at wayside accommodations. After the Rebel agents finally brought A'kazz to B'karitz—and convinced the mistrustful [[Noble/Legends|noble]] of their good intentions—the latter [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] interrogated the child. What he learned from A'kazz made B'karitz vow to prevent the further construction of the Imperial base on Rhamsis Callo, break the treaty with the Empire, and drive the Imperials from the planet.<ref name="TRG1" />

The nest-guildmaster, accompanied by both his [[nest-warrior]]s and the Rebel group, traveled to the partially constructed Imperial base, where B'karitz summoned the Empire's [[Unidentified Imperial ambassador to Rhamsis Callo|ambassador]] and declared to them that the treaty between the Empire and the Schenor was null and void and that the Empire's presence on Rhamsis Callo was to end immediately. Although the Imperials pretended to cooperate, they used a secret alarm to summon [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], who then surrounded and attacked B'karitz and his force. The Rebels and the nest-guildmaster boarded the former's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], from where B'karitz summoned aid from other Schenor nobles. The Alliance agents then engaged the Imperial forces, including fleeing starships, in further combat. The Rebels disabled and boarded the [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador's]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], which then allowed them to send the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] back to Rhamsis Callo to face Schenor justice.<ref name="TRG1" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Schenor}}
Rhamsis Callo was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Schenor, a species of eight-[[Foot/Legends|foot]]-tall [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and telepathic [[Feline/Legends|felinoid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]]. The Schenor society, organized into units known as nest-guilds, had also stratified into tiers of lowly [[Farmer/Legends|peasants]] and middle-class as well as all-powerful nobles. The natives had a government representing the species in relations with off-world visitors—of whom they were traditionally distrustful—and their homeworld's units of territorial administration included provinces.<ref name="TRG1" />

The militaristic Schenor lived according to a rigid code of honor, and martial arts were held in respect in their society. The transportation technology of Rhamsis Callo's natives included reaction cars, a type of powerful and fast, but also noisy, inefficient, and dangerous, [[Hovercraft|ground-effect vehicles]]. At some point, the Schenor established a remote research station [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] off-world. In turn, after the species established contact with the Galactic Empire, an Imperial contingent including an ambassador and troopers as well as several starships was dispatched to Rhamsis Callo.<ref name="TRG1" />

==Locations==
The Schenor architecture of Rhamsis Callo, which included the [[Rhamsis Callo Spaceport]] at [[Chafflock]], consisted of [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Sandstone/Legends|sandstone]] buildings decorated with geometric ornaments made of earth-tone [[Clay/Legends|clay]]. In addition, during the Galactic Civil War, the Galactic Empire was constructing a military base on the planet.<ref name="TRG1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tion-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|200px|Map from ''Voyages SF'' 13 depicting Rhamsis Callo as part of the Tion Cluster.]]
Rhamsis Callo first appeared in the [[roleplaying game]] adventure idea "[[The Silent Witness]]," which was authored by [[Ken Rolston]] and published in the [[1987]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|rulebook]] for the first edition of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="TRG1" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] roleplaying source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the planet in the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|"Tion" sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Rhamsis Callo [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Rhamsis Callo itself, in grid square N-19, far from the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]<ref name="TEA" /> and the trio of [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] into which it was divided.<ref name="Xim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the system was situated in the Danjar sector.<ref name="EAOC" />
